title: Quiz: The Integer Factoring Problem
description: Test your understanding of integer factorization and its importance

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Shor's algorithm for factoring an n-bit integer?
questions.explanation: Shor's algorithm runs in O(n³) time, which is polynomial. This is exponentially faster than the best known classical algorithms.

questions.question: RSA encryption security is based on the difficulty of:
questions.explanation: RSA security relies on the difficulty of factoring N = p × q where p and q are large primes (a semiprime).

questions.question: The best classical factoring algorithm (GNFS) has what type of complexity?
questions.explanation: GNFS (General Number Field Sieve) is subexponential: O(exp(c·n^(1/3)·(log n)^(2/3))). It's faster than exponential but slower than polynomial.

questions.question: When was Shor's algorithm discovered?
questions.explanation: Peter Shor discovered his algorithm in 1994, revolutionizing our understanding of quantum computational power.

questions.question: Multiplying two large primes is just as hard as factoring their product.
questions.explanation: This asymmetry is the foundation of RSA! Multiplication is easy (polynomial time), while factoring is hard (believed to require exponential time classically).
